body_txt: This is for when a character says or does something clearly contradictory, if not completely opposite, to what they literally said right beforehand. May sometimes happen in the context of a Hurricane of Excuses . Can be caused by a Distinction Without a Difference . When a contradiction seems immediate to the viewer but is not immediate in in-universe time, that would be a Gilligan Cut . Compare Confession Deferred (confessing the truth only when it's convenient). See also Verbal Backspace , I Resemble That Remark! , Ironic Echo Cut , Everything Except Most Things . Subtrope of Hypocritical Humor . May result in Explain, Explain... Oh, Crap! For instances where a character is contradicted by someone/something else, see Instantly Proven Wrong . If the speaker waits until their audience is out of earshot to contradict themselves, that's Two-Faced Aside . See also Stereotype Reaction Gag , By "No", I Mean "Yes" , Double Think . This is often the case in Such a Phony situations, when one character talks crap about another but pretends to be nice in their presence. When it occurs in a single phrase, this is known as an "Irish bull", from that nation's alleged predilection for it. (E.g., Sir Boyle Roche: "My love for England and Ireland is such that I would see the two sisters embrace as one brother.")
